Per page 39 of the eMedNY Prospective Drug Utilization Review/Electronic Claims Capture and Adjudication ProDUR/ECCA Provider Manual, located at: https://www.emedny.org/ProviderManuals/ Pharmacy/ProDUR-D.0-ECCA_Provider_Manual/Pro%20DUR%20ECCA%20Provider%20Manual%20 (D. ...)",

The three types of Medicare enrollment include CMS-460 Medicare ...]",
  "similarities": ["Similarity: Pharmacy claims are subject to Medicaid requirements.", "[New York State Medicaid Update April 2023 Volume 39 Number 8] — [Chunk ID: mu_no8_apr23_pr.pdf::0021] — [2023-04-01] - [pages: [9]]"],

  "caveats": "None"
}

{
  "headline_summary": "According to the newest provider manual source dated October 1, 2025, Medicaid is not responsible for billing Medicare Part D-excluded drugs. In contrast, the policy guidance from April 2025 states that Medicaid will cover certain prescription drugs not covered by Medicare Part D, but this is not explicitly stated as a co-payment responsibility for dual-eligible members.",
  "policy_definition": "[New York State Medicaid Update April 2025 Volume 41 Number 4] — [Chunk ID: mu_no04_apr25_pr.pdf::0026]-[Effective Date: 2025-04-01]-[pages: [8]] - Certain services may be rejected by the primary payer, but coverage should still be pursued in situations including, but not limited to: • prior authorization (PA) needs to be obtained from the primary payer; • billing Medicare Part D for services covered by Medicare Part B, or billing Medicare Part B for services covered by Medicare Part D and getting a rejection; • submitting to the wrong third-party causing claims to bypass incorrectly.",
  "provider_manual_definition": "[New York State Medicaid Program: Pharmacy Manual - Policy Guidelines] -[Chunk ID: Pharmacy_Policy_Guidelines.pdf::0125]-[Effective Date: 2025-10-01]-[pages: [30, 31]] - All prescription drugs are reimbursed on a fee-for-service basis. Residential health care facilities may: • Operate an institutional pharmacy to provide these items or • Contract with Medicaid enrolled pharmacies to provide these items to Medicaid members. The pharmacy must be reimbursed by the facility for all non-prescription drugs and medical/surgical supplies.",
  "similarities": ["Both sources indicate that Medicaid should pursue coverage for certain prescription drugs not covered by Medicare Part D.", "[New York State Medicaid Update April 2025 Volume 41 Number 4] -[Chunk ID: mu_no04_apr25_pr.pdf::0026]-[Effective Date: 2025-04-01]-[pages: [8]]"],
  "differences": ["The policy guidance from April 2025 does not explicitly state co-payment responsibility for dual-eligible members.", "[New York State Medicaid Program: Pharmacy Manual - Policy Guidelines] -[Chunk ID: Pharmacy_Policy_Guidelines.pdf::0125]-[Effective Date: 2025-10-01]-[pages: [30, 31]]"],
  "caveats": "None"
}

The pharmacy must have documentation confirming the prescription number(s), date of pick-up and signature. One signature is sufficient for multiple prescriptions being picked up at one time. Claim submission is not required to obtain a signature. However, if the address, age, or CIN of the Medicaid member are missing, the pharmacist is not required to enter any of these items on the prescription if the information: • Is otherwise readily available in the records of the pharmacy and the pharmacist knows the person who is requesting that the prescription be filled, or • The pharmacist is otherwise satisfied that the prescription is legitimate.
